methods.description: Process for collecting geological samples from various sites for cosmogenic nuclide analysis.

steps.description: At each site, the coordinates of the sampling locations were determined using a GPS and the altitudes were taken from the high resolution digital maps, made publicly available by the Federal Office of Topography (swisstopo), because they are more accurate compared to the ones measured by the GPS.
steps.searchable_summary: determine sampling location coordinates using GPS and swisstopo maps

steps.description: At each site, at least nine clasts and a sediment sample consisting of > 50 quartz pebbles were collected.
steps.searchable_summary: collect clasts and sediment samples from each site

steps.description: To ensure a variety of pre-burial histories, we collected clasts of different lithologies, shapes, and sizes.
steps.searchable_summary: collect diverse clasts for varied pre-burial histories

methods.method_name: Sample Preparation for Cosmogenic Nuclide Analysis
methods.description: Preparation of geological samples for cosmogenic 10Be and 26Al analyses, including purification and measurement processes.

steps.description: For each study site, we first prioritized nine clasts and the sediment samples and purified them according to the methods described by Akçar et al., (2017 and references therein).
steps.searchable_summary: purify clasts and sediment samples for analysis

steps.description: We aimed for approximately 50 g of pure quartz. To achieve high quality 26Al/27Al accelerator mass spectrometry (AMS) measurements a maximum Al concentration of 30 ppm was targeted prior to dissolution.
steps.searchable_summary: ensure pure quartz and low Al concentration for AMS
outputs: samples with target quartz and Al concentration

steps.description: Samples that exceeded the targeted Al concentration were either subjected to additional leaching steps or excluded.
steps.searchable_summary: perform additional leaching for samples with high Al concentration

steps.description: The samples fulfilling the requirements were dissolved in batches of nine samples and a full process blank.
steps.searchable_summary: dissolve samples in batches for analysis

steps.description: Each sample was spiked with ca. 200 µl of Be carrier and the full process blank with 400 µl.

The cosmogenic 10Be and 26Al were extracted based on the protocols described by Akçar et al., (2017), after which the 10Be/9Be ratios and 26Al/27Al ratios were measured at the AMS facilities at ETH Zurich.
steps.searchable_summary: extract and measure cosmogenic nuclide ratios using AMS

steps.description: All measured 10Be/9Be ratios were normalized to the ETH Zurich in-house standards S2007N and S2010N and corrected with a weighted average full process blank ratio of (2.76 ± 0.18) × 10^-15.
steps.searchable_summary: normalize and correct 10Be/9Be ratios using standards

steps.description: The total Al concentrations were determined using inductively coupled plasma optical emission spectrometry (ICP-OES) at the Institute of Geological Sciences of the University of Bern.
steps.searchable_summary: determine total Al concentrations using ICP-OES
